claims.summary: Phase witnesses and felt receipts make transition experience distinct from telemetry completion.
claims.disposition: Verified current phase, witness, agency-commons, and felt-contract surfaces retain that distinction as evidence only.

claims.summary: Telemetry can look smooth while Astrid still experiences a hollow or unwitnessed transition.
claims.disposition: Preserved as a first-class possible concordance outcome; a numeric pass cannot overwrite, close, or weaken the felt report.

claims.summary: A mismatch probe should retain qualitative and mechanical divergence without forcing reconciliation.
claims.disposition: Verified Felt-Mechanism Concordance already supports mechanism_smooth_felt_friction_remains and temporal labeling without causal or closure propagation.

claims.summary: A saturated right-to-ignore budget should not erase or penalize a phase report.
claims.disposition: Verified review-budget and neutral-silence tests hold duplicate delivery while retaining queryability, evidence history, and immediate objection or still-friction reopening.

claims.summary: A numeric discrepancy score should quantify the gap between systemic stability and subjective continuity.
claims.disposition: Verified the experiential epistemics boundary intentionally forbids felt scoring. The same distinction is retained qualitatively and can be mechanically contextualized without a scalar felt delta.
